arguments.task: Research MongoDB Atlas using official first-party sources. Visit MongoDB Atlas API docs, authentication docs, Atlas Administration API reference or overview pages, Atlas pricing/access pages, and any official MongoDB GitHub or MCP-related pages if available. Extract exact evidence on: available API surface types (REST or GraphQL), authentication methods for the Atlas API, whether access is self-serve or requires paid/admin/partner approval, any rate limit notes, and signals about existing MCP server availability. Prefer official developer docs, pricing pages, support docs, changelogs, and official GitHub pages. Return concise findings with source URLs and quoted supporting text.

Search for official MongoDB GitHub repositories or docs that mention an MCP server for MongoDB Atlas or MongoDB. Verify whether there is an official MongoDB MCP server or documentation. Prefer official GitHub org repos and official docs pages. Return source URLs and short evidence snippets.

input.thought: Launch parallel browser research tasks for official MongoDB Atlas docs and official MCP/GitHub evidence.

Return a compact list of findings with exact URLs and short quotes.

raw_result.description: Cloud database platform and control plane for deploying, managing, and monitoring MongoDB clusters and data services.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official Atlas Administration API docs indicate OAuth 2.0 via service accounts and legacy API keys; the authentication page also references HTTP Digest Authentication for legacy API keys, which is captured under other due to schema constraints.
raw_result.gating_reason: MongoDB Atlas has public documentation and self-serve access paths, including public pricing/free-tier pages; however, some Atlas Administration API actions require Organization Owner or Project Owner roles.

api_surface.breadth: Public Atlas Administration API with versioned REST endpoints (v1/v2) for Atlas org/project/cluster administration; evidence gathered did not confirm any GraphQL surface for Atlas Administration API.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official MongoDB MCP Server exists via official docs and GitHub; evidence says it supports MongoDB databases and MongoDB Atlas clusters, including Atlas tools.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official Atlas API rate-limit docs indicate a token bucket algorithm and document default rate limits including a 1200 bucket capacity for certain GROUP scopes and a 500 requests per 60 seconds refill rate; 429 is used when limits are exceeded. Exact per-scope coverage should be human-verified from the rate-limit table.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official first-party docs cover Atlas Administration API auth, access configuration, and rate limits. Official MongoDB MCP server docs and repository are available, strengthening agent-tooling viability. Docs also note role prerequisites for some API configuration tasks.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Some details were inferred from browser-captured snippets rather than directly quoted in-chat: exact pricing/free-tier wording, exact legacy auth labeling (API key plus HTTP Digest), and whether Atlas has any separate GraphQL surface outside the gathered Administration API evidence should be human-verified.
